description_long: This is a roughly oval-shaped stone with a predominantly pale, off-white coloration and a slightly flattened profile. The front face features a large, smooth, lighter oval patch that contrasts with the rougher, more irregular surrounding surface. The sides are relatively straight and vertical, with visible chisel or break marks, suggesting it may have been shaped or split from a larger rock. The back is more uniformly textured and lacks the distinct central patch seen on the front. The stone's surface is matte and shows minor pitting and natural imperfections, indicating some exposure to the elements but not extensive weathering. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a low barrier or boundary marker in an urban park or along a sidewalk.
description: A pale, oval-shaped stone with a smooth central patch on one face and rough, straight sides, showing minor natural imperfections.
description_view_0: The front view displays a large, smooth, lighter oval patch set within a rougher, off-white stone surface.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the stone's straight, vertical edge with visible break marks and a narrow profile.
description_view_2: The back view shows a more uniformly rough and pale surface, lacking the distinct central patch of the front.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again highlights the straight, vertical edge and the stone's overall thickness.
attribute_location: urban park border or sidewalk edge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or boundary marker on the ground
attribute_pattern: A large, smooth, lighter oval patch on the front face contrasts with the rougher, irregular stone surface; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Minor surface pitting and imperfections, indicating some exposure but not extensive weathering; overall matte and unpolished.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically placed to contrast gently with paved surfaces or grass, serving as a visual and physical boundary; may partially blend with other natural stones if grouped.
